steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content:
The sentence contains two independent clauses:
(1) “The sand at Komodo Island Beach in Indonesia is a more unusual shade”
(2) “deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue”
These must be connected with proper punctuation. The word “shade” completes the first clause, and we need punctuation + “though” to bridge to the second.

steps.title: Apply the rule for discourse markers between independent clauses
steps.content:
”Though” here functions as a discourse marker (a concession like “well, that said” or “regardless”), interrupting between two ICs.

The punctuation rule is: use a comma after the first IC to separate the marker, then a semicolon to join the second IC.

This creates the pattern: IC, though; IC. That matches choice A — A is correct.
